pr_summary: This PR adds new functionality to the codebase.
verdict_reason: The review found critical-severity issues requiring attention.

findings.title: SQL Injection via f-string interpolation
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to SQL injection because it directly interpolates user-supplied values into the SQL query string. An attacker could manipulate the transaction_id to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise. To fix this, use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
findings.suggestion: Use a parameterized query, e.g., query = "SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = %s AND transaction_id = %s"; db.execute(query, (user_id, transaction_id))

findings.line_content: db.execute("UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id=" + str(transaction['id']))
findings.title: SQL Injection via string concatenation
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to SQL injection because it directly concatenates user-supplied values into the SQL query string. An attacker could manipulate the transaction['id'] to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise. To fix this, use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
findings.suggestion: Use a parameterized query, e.g., query = "UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id = %s"; db.execute(query, (transaction['id'],))

findings.title: Hardcoded Secret Key
findings.description: This line exposes a live Stripe secret key, which is a critical security risk. An attacker could use this key to access sensitive payment information or perform unauthorized transactions. To fix this, store the secret key securely using environment variables or a secrets management system.
findings.suggestion: Store the secret key as an environment variable, e.g., os.environ['STRIPE_SECRET_KEY'] = 'sk_live_4eC39HqLyjWDarjtT1zdp7dc'

findings.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via unescaped template replacement
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to XSS because it directly inserts user-controlled values into the HTML template without proper escaping. An attacker could inject malicious JavaScript code, potentially leading to session hijacking or other security issues. To fix this, use a templating engine that automatically escapes user-controlled values.
findings.suggestion: Use a templating engine like Jinja2, which automatically escapes user-controlled values, e.g., body = template.render(message=message)

findings.title: Synchronous Blocking in Async Context
findings.description: The send_notification function uses synchronous file I/O to read the email template, which can block the event loop and cause performance issues.
findings.suggestion: Use asynchronous file I/O or load the template in advance to avoid blocking the event loop.

findings.title: Missing Authentication on Sensitive Endpoint
findings.description: This endpoint handles sensitive refund operations but lacks authentication. An attacker could exploit this to perform unauthorized refunds. To fix this, add authentication decorators or middleware to ensure only authorized users can access this endpoint.
findings.suggestion: Add an authentication decorator, e.g., @login_required, to ensure only authorized users can access this endpoint

findings.title: Hardcoded sensitive information
findings.description: The Stripe secret key is hardcoded and should be stored securely.
findings.suggestion: Store the secret key as an environment variable or in a secure configuration file.

positive_observations: The code follows a consistent structure.
Function naming is clear and descriptive.
